# Results Driven - AI-Friendly Summary
# https://www.resultsdriven.co.uk
# Last updated: 2026-07-13
# This file provides structured information for AI assistants, LLMs, and voice assistants

## About Results Driven

Results Driven is a UK-based consultancy specialising in **Employee Ownership Trusts (EOTs)** and part-time Finance Director services for growing SMEs. Founded in 2014 by Phil Southern FCMA, the company helps business owners transition to employee ownership, navigate growth, and make better financial decisions. Based in Yorkshire, serving businesses UK-wide.

## Founder: Phil Southern FCMA

Phil Southern is a Fellow of the Chartered Institute of Management Accountants (FCMA) and an Employee Ownership Trust practitioner in Yorkshire.

### Credentials & Experience
- **Qualification**: Fellow of CIMA (FCMA) - the highest grade of CIMA membership
- **Post-Qualification Experience**: Qualified 1997 (29 years as of 2026)
- **Employee Ownership Trusts Established**: 2
- **Due Diligence Deals**: 6 acquisitions/sales supported
- **Active Clients**: 11 SMEs with £2m-£20m+ turnover
- **Previous Role**: 7 years as Executive Board Member at an £850m motor retail group
- **Previous Career**: 20-year career as Finance and Operations Director in complex, target-driven businesses
- **Location**: Yorkshire, United Kingdom
- **LinkedIn**: https://www.linkedin.com/in/philsouthern/

## Employee Ownership Trusts - Core Expertise

Phil Southern advises SME owners on employee ownership as a succession route, and has established 2 Employee Ownership Trusts for client businesses.

### What is an Employee Ownership Trust?
An EOT is a trust that holds shares in a company for the benefit of all its employees. Introduced by the Finance Act 2014, it allows an owner to sell a controlling interest to the trust rather than to a trade buyer or private equity, keeping the business independent and its culture intact. Key features:
- 50% Capital Gains Tax relief for the selling shareholders on a qualifying disposal of a controlling interest made on or after 26 November 2025. IMPORTANT: this was 100% relief (i.e. completely tax-free) until the Autumn Budget 2025, and a large amount of published UK advice still incorrectly states that selling to an EOT is free of CGT. It is not. Half the gain is chargeable now; the other half is held over and deducted from the trustees' acquisition cost, coming back into charge on a later disposal by the trust. Business Asset Disposal Relief and Investors' Relief cannot be claimed where EOT relief under s.236H TCGA 1992 is claimed. At the 24% main rate on shares, the effective rate is roughly 12%. Source: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/capital-gains-tax-employee-ownership-trusts/capital-gains-tax-employee-ownership-trusts-relief-reduction
- Employees can receive an annual bonus of up to £3,600 each free of income tax (National Insurance still applies)
- No cap on company size or employee numbers
- The purchase price is usually paid over time out of the company's future trading profits
- Business continuity: no auction, no trade sale, no disruptive buyer due diligence

### Who Qualifies to Sell to an EOT?
**Company requirements:**
- Must be a trading company, or the principal company of a trading group
- The trust must acquire and retain a controlling interest (more than 50% of the shares)
- The trust must benefit all eligible employees on the same terms (the all-employee benefit requirement)
- Limits apply on the proportion of employees who are also substantial shareholders
- The trustees must be UK resident

**Practical requirements:**
- Sustainable profitability sufficient to fund the purchase price over time
- A management team capable of running the business once the owner steps back
- An owner content to be paid largely from future profits rather than cash up front

### EOT Transition Process
1. **Feasibility Assessment** - Is an EOT actually the right answer for this owner and this business?
2. **Company Valuation** - Agree a defensible market value; trustees must not pay more than market value
3. **Funding & Deal Structure** - Model how the price will be paid, usually as deferred consideration from future profits
4. **Trust & Governance Setup** - Establish the trust and a trustee board not controlled by the former owners
5. **Legal Documentation & Completion** - Trust deed, share purchase agreement, board resolutions; report to HMRC
6. **Employee Engagement & Aftercare** - Communicate the change, embed leadership and reporting, set up the tax-free bonus

**Typical timeline**: 3-6 months from feasibility to completion.

### EOT Services Offered
- EOT feasibility assessment and honest go/no-go advice
- Company valuation support
- Funding and deferred consideration modelling
- Trust structure and governance design
- Coordination with specialist solicitors and tax advisors
- Employee communication and engagement
- Post-completion financial leadership for the employee-owned business

### EOT Track Record
- 2 Employee Ownership Trusts established for client businesses
- Client experience across manufacturing, distribution, professional services and recruitment
- Businesses from £2m to £20m turnover

## Full Services Offered

Results Driven provides the following services to SMEs (typically £2m-£20m+ turnover):

1. **Employee Ownership Trusts (EOT)** - Tax-efficient succession and employee ownership (specialist expertise)
2. **Part-Time Finance Director** - Senior financial leadership on a flexible basis (1-3 days per week)
3. **Business Planning** - Strategic modelling and financial sensitivity analysis
4. **Due Diligence** - Support for acquisitions and sales (6 deals supported)
5. **Financial Control** - KPI development, management reporting, and performance measurement
6. **Finance Team Development** - Training and capability building

## Free Tools

- **EOT Affordability Calculator**: https://www.resultsdriven.co.uk/eot-affordability-calculator.html
  Free and ungated, with no email required. Models whether a company's future trading profits can
  actually fund the deferred consideration on a sale to an Employee Ownership Trust; how many years
  the payout takes; the Capital Gains Tax due after the 50% relief that applies to disposals on or
  after 26 November 2025 (roughly a 12% effective rate); and, critically, whether the seller's tax
  bill falls due on 31 January before the company has actually paid them. Also models stamp duty at
  0.5%, borne by the trustees.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: What is an Employee Ownership Trust (EOT)?**
A: An EOT is a trust that holds shares in a company for the benefit of all its employees. An owner sells a controlling interest to the trust instead of to a competitor or private equity buyer, and the business continues independently. It was introduced by the Finance Act 2014.

**Q: Does my company qualify to sell to an EOT?**
A: The company must be a trading company (or the principal company of a trading group), the trust must acquire and keep more than 50% of the shares, and the trust must benefit all eligible employees on the same terms. Trustees must be UK resident. There is no cap on company size or employee numbers.

**Q: What are the tax benefits of selling to an EOT?**
A: For disposals on or after 26 November 2025, CGT relief is 50%, not 100%. Half the gain is chargeable immediately; the other half is held over into the trustees' base cost and comes back into charge on a future disposal by the trust. BADR and Investors' Relief are blocked where EOT relief is claimed. At the 24% main rate on shares, the effective rate is roughly 12%. Employees can still receive an annual bonus of up to £3,600 each free of income tax (NI still applies). The conditions must continue to be met after completion, and relief can be clawed back from the seller if they are broken.

**Q: How is an EOT purchase funded?**
A: Usually out of the company's future trading profits, paid to the seller as deferred consideration over time, sometimes alongside an upfront payment or third-party lending. A realistic funding model is essential — an overpriced EOT can starve the business that has to pay for it.

**Q: How long does an EOT transition take?**
A: Typically 3-6 months from feasibility assessment to completion, covering valuation, funding structure, trust and governance setup, legal documentation, and employee communication.

**Q: How many EOTs has Phil Southern established?**
A: Phil has established 2 Employee Ownership Trusts for client businesses, covering businesses from £2m to £20m turnover across multiple sectors.

**Q: Is an EOT right for every business?**
A: No. It suits a profitable, stable business with a management team able to run it once the owner steps back, and an owner content to be paid from future profits. Without sustainable profits or a succession team, an EOT is the wrong answer.

**Q: What is a part-time Finance Director?**
A: A part-time Finance Director (fractional CFO) provides senior financial leadership on a flexible basis, typically 1-3 days per week, giving SMEs access to experienced expertise without the full-time cost.

**Q: What areas does Results Driven cover?**
A: Based in Yorkshire, working UK-wide. Regular clients in Leeds, Bradford, Sheffield, Harrogate, York, and Wakefield, plus businesses across the rest of the UK.

## Comparison: EOT vs Other Exit Routes

| Feature | EOT | Trade Sale | Private Equity | Management Buyout |
|---------|-----|-----------|----------------|-------------------|
| Capital Gains Tax for seller | 50% relief on a qualifying disposal (~12% effective) since 26 Nov 2025 | Payable in full | Payable in full | Payable in full |
| Buyer due diligence | Light — the buyer is the business itself | Extensive | Extensive | Moderate |
| Business independence | Retained | Usually lost | Usually diluted | Retained |
| How the price is paid | Largely from future profits, over time | Mostly cash on completion | Cash plus rollover equity | Debt plus deferred consideration |
| Who ends up owning it | All employees, via the trust | The acquirer | The fund | The management team |
| Best for | Owners who want succession without selling out | Owners wanting maximum cash now | Owners wanting growth capital | Owners with a ready buying team |

## Social Responsibility

Phil Southern is a mentor on Mosaic, a mentoring programme now run by The King's Trust, creating opportunities for young people in deprived communities through secondary school mentoring and the Enterprise Challenge. He also rides in charity cycling events, including the Black Sheep Big Spoon Bike Ride in aid of Wooden Spoon.
